title: Please enhance the quality of this email, specifically the section regarding why I enjoyed the class. Additionally, plea…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Please enhance the quality of this email, specifically the section regarding why I enjoyed the class. Additionally, please rephrase the question to ask if the recommender would be able to write a letter of recommendation rather than asking to meet. Understand that this is from the point of view of a student requesting a letter of recommendation for medical school, and it should be a professional and polite request. The email is as follows: I hope this email finds you well! I am writing to you because I am currently in the process of preparing my application for medical school and was hoping you would be able to write a letter of recommendation on my behalf. The due date for the letter is July 1, 2023. I have also attached my resume to this email for your reference.

I thoroughly enjoyed taking your Biochemical Methods I (Chem 153L) course during the Spring 2021 quarter. Although I was unable to perform any of the labs in person, I enjoyed your engaging lectures and lab section activities that helped me learn how to compile, analyze, and synthesize a report that answers a research question. I also particularly enjoyed learning how to critically examine scientific literature and present my analysis to others, a skill which I have found particularly useful to this day.

As someone who has played an integral part of my academic journey and witnessed my dedication to learning, I believe that a letter of recommendation from you would carry significant weight and help strengthen my medical school application. If you are willing to write a strong letter of recommendation for me, would it be possible to meet sometime this week, or at your earliest convenience, to discuss any additional information that may be beneficial for the letter?

I understand you are very busy, but I appreciate your time, consideration, and knowledge that you have imparted with me.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Subject: Request for a Letter of Recommendation for Medical School Application

Dear [Professor's Name],

I hope this email finds you in good health and high spirits! I am currently in the process of preparing my application for medical school, and I am reaching out to inquire if you would be willing and able to provide a letter of recommendation on my behalf. The due date for the letter is July 1, 2023. For your convenience and reference, I have attached my resume to this email.

Your Biochemical Methods I (Chem 153L) course that I attended during the Spring 2021 quarter has been a highlight of my academic experience. Despite the inability to conduct labs in person, your engaging lectures and lab section activities enabled me to develop crucial skills in compiling, analyzing, and synthesizing reports that address research questions. Moreover, the opportunity to critically assess scientific literature and effectively communicate the analysis to others has proven invaluable in my academic journey thus far.

Considering the significant role you have played in my academic development and your firsthand experience with my dedication to learning, I believe that a recommendation letter from you would greatly enhance my medical school application. If you are available and inclined to write a strong letter of recommendation on my behalf, might there be an opportunity for us to connect in the near future to discuss any additional information that could be beneficial for the letter?

I fully understand the constraints on your time, and I am immensely grateful for your attention to my request, as well as for the wisdom and knowledge you have shared with me throughout my studies.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Make this flow well and sound professional, as it is for a college professor who taught the course: 
I thoroughly enjoyed taking your Biochemical Methods I (Chem 153L) course during the Spring 2021 quarter. Despite the inability to conduct labs in person, your engaging lectures and lab section activities helped me learn to compile and analyze experimental data. Particularly, the examples of optimizing YqhD overexpression and comparing its isobutanol production to other alcohol dehydrogenases helped me understand the process of creating conclusions from data to help answer a research question. Additionally, the opportunity to critically assess scientific literature and present my analysis to others has proven invaluable in my academic journey.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
It was a delight to have taken your Biochemical Methods I (Chem 153L) course during the Spring 2021 quarter. Despite the limitations posed by not being able to conduct labs in person, your captivating lectures and well-structured lab section activities provided me with the necessary skills to compile and analyze experimental data effectively. The examples of optimizing YqhD overexpression and contrasting its isobutanol production to that of other alcohol dehydrogenases served as an exemplary model, allowing me to better comprehend the process of drawing meaningful conclusions from data to address research questions.

Furthermore, the opportunity to critically evaluate scientific literature and share my insights with others has proved to be invaluable in my academic journey thus far. Your guidance through these experiences has significantly contributed to the development of my analytical prowess and effective communication skills, which I believe are essential for success in the medical field.
